title: The ESAs support ESRB warning on systemic cyber risks from frontier AI models
summary: The EBA, EIOPA, and ESMA have issued a joint statement welcoming and supporting an ESRB warning on the systemic cyber risks posed by frontier AI models, which are enabling threat actors to identify and exploit high-severity vulnerabilities at greater speed and scale. The ESAs urge financial entities to strengthen their cybersecurity capabilities and invite competent authorities to reflect these developments in their supervisory activities, noting that existing frameworks including DORA and the AI Act provide a foundation but may be challenged by the pace of AI-enabled threats. The ESAs also state they are engaging with Critical ICT Third-Party Providers as Overseers under DORA and will work with national supervisors to clarify and communicate supervisory expectations.

title: ​The EBA publishes 2025 loss data for immovable property markets under Article 430a of the Capital Requirements Regulation
summary: The EBA has published its annual 2025 dataset on losses and exposures for residential and commercial immovable property across the EU/EEA, as required by Article 430a(3) of the Capital Requirements Regulation. The data support application of the CRR 'hard test' mechanism, enabling certain preferential treatments for immovable property exposures under Articles 125, 126, and 199 where loss-rate thresholds are met. The publication does not amend legal conditions or constitute a supervisory decision; institutions remain responsible for assessing whether CRR conditions are met.

title: Targeted EBA peer review finds high compliance on Pillar 3 disclosures but calls for greater consistency
summary: The EBA has published the results of a targeted peer review assessing how competent authorities supervise compliance with Pillar 3 disclosure requirements under the CRR and BRRD, covering the period 1 June 2023 to 30 June 2025. The review found that most competent authorities have fully or largely embedded Pillar 3 requirements into their supervisory frameworks, though one authority was rated 'partially applied' and another received mostly 'not applied' ratings due to the absence of formal methodologies. The EBA calls for greater consistency across jurisdictions and notes that corrective actions are already underway at some authorities.

title: The EBA launches consultation on key performance indicators of Taxonomy disclosures
summary: The EBA has published a Discussion Paper consulting on potential simplifications and enhancements to key performance indicators (KPIs) under Article 8 of the Taxonomy Regulation's Disclosures Delegated Act, targeting credit institutions and investment firms. The consultation responds to a March 2026 Call for Technical Advice from the European Commission and covers topics including simplification of specific KPIs, grandfathering provisions, and group-level disclosures. Comments are due by 12 August 2026, with the ESAs expected to submit their advice to the European Commission in October 2026.

title: The EBA clarifies its Product Oversight and Governance Guidelines which address greenwashing risks in ESG products
summary: The EBA has published revised Guidelines on product oversight and governance (POG) for retail banking products, clarifying requirements for products with ESG features and explicitly addressing greenwashing risks throughout the product lifecycle. The amendments target manufacturers' internal controls, target market identification, distribution channels, and information provided to distributors, while also removing outdated provisions to align with the current regulatory framework. The Guidelines apply from 11 January 2027 and are addressed to manufacturers and distributors of products within the EBA's remit, including mortgages, personal loans, deposits, payment accounts, payment services, and electronic money.

title: EU deposit guarantee scheme funds to protect depositors against bank failures continue to grow and have reached a volume of €85bn, the EBA observes
summary: The EBA has published end-2025 data on EU deposit guarantee schemes (DGSs), showing that aggregate available funds reached €85bn, a 4.9% increase, and that all but one of the 33 EU DGSs have met the legally required minimum target level of 0.8% of covered deposits. Covered deposits grew 2.9% to €9.1tn between 2024 and 2025. The annual publication covers all 27 EU Member States plus Iceland, Norway, and Liechtenstein, and is issued under Article 10(10) of the DGSD.

title: The EBA publishes its 2025 Report on supervisory convergence, supporting a simpler and more efficient EU prudential framework
summary: The EBA has published its 2025 Report on Supervisory Convergence, covering progress in aligning supervisory practices across EU Member States in areas including prudential supervision, resolution, digital finance, consumer protection, and AML/CFT. Key 2025 developments include advances in resilience testing, DORA and MiCA rollout support, bail-in readiness, and the development of an EU-wide payment fraud database. Looking ahead to 2026, the EBA identifies continued focus on Basel III implementation, resolution testing, DORA oversight, and MiCA supervision as priorities.

title: The EBA publishes a roadmap on the delivery of its mandates under the revised Deposit Guarantee Schemes Directive
summary: The EBA has published a roadmap detailing how it will deliver its mandates under the revised Deposit Guarantee Schemes Directive (DGSD3), which was published on 20 April 2026 and applies from May 2028. The roadmap covers 12 regulatory products to be delivered in three batches over three years, addressing depositor information, repayment speed, cross-border cooperation, and stress-testing of deposit guarantee schemes. It provides stakeholders with clarity on the sequencing of the EBA's work ahead of the Directive's application date.

title: ​The European Banking Authority consults on a draft methodology for setting fines under the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA)
summary: The EBA has published a Consultation Paper proposing a methodology for calculating fines against issuers of significant asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) and e-money tokens (EMTs) under MiCA, in its capacity as direct supervisor of such issuers. The methodology aims to ensure fines are consistent, proportionate, and transparent where an issuer or a member of its management body has negligently or intentionally committed an infringement. Comments are due by 28 September 2026, with a virtual public hearing scheduled for 16 July 2026.

title: ​The EBA updates validation rules for supervisory reporting ​
summary: The EBA has published an updated list of validation rules for its supervisory reporting frameworks as part of its regular quarterly review process, identifying rules that have been deactivated, reactivated, or changed in severity status. Competent Authorities are reminded not to formally validate data against deactivated rules. The update also includes a micro taxonomy package and Data Point Model validation rules update scripts required from release 4.0 onwards, reflecting the integration of validation rules into both the taxonomy and DPM under DPM 2.0.

title: The ESAs support ESRB warning on systemic cyber risks from frontier AI models
summary: The ESAs (EBA, EIOPA and ESMA) have issued a joint statement welcoming and supporting an ESRB warning on the systemic cyber risks posed by frontier AI models, which are increasingly capable of identifying and exploiting high-severity IT vulnerabilities at speed and scale. The ESAs urge all EU financial entities to strengthen their cybersecurity capabilities and invite competent authorities to reflect these risks in their supervisory activities, framing compliance within the existing DORA framework. No immediate compliance deadline is specified, but the ESAs indicate they will work with national supervisors to clarify and communicate supervisory expectations.

title: ESMA selects Etrading Software (Netherlands) B.V. as Consolidated Tape Provider for OTC derivatives
summary: ESMA has selected Etrading Software (Netherlands) B.V. as the Consolidated Tape Provider (CTP) for OTC derivatives under MiFIR, following a comprehensive assessment of its application against criteria including data quality, resilience, and consolidated market data dissemination. ESMA will invite the selected entity to apply for authorisation without delay, after which it will operate the consolidated tape for OTC derivatives for five years under ESMA's direct supervision. No immediate compliance deadline is imposed on market participants by this announcement.

title: ESMA publishes preliminary findings on the Active Account Requirement and the first Annual Report of the Joint Monitoring Mechanism
summary: ESMA has published an Interim Report on the effectiveness of the Active Account Requirement (AAR) under EMIR, covering implementation during 2025 and early 2026, finding that around 500 entities had notified ESMA and national competent authorities of their AAR obligations, with early signs of increased clearing activity at EU CCPs. ESMA has also published the first Annual Report of the Joint Monitoring Mechanism, which monitors financial stability risks across EU CCPs, clearing members and clients, and identifies cross-border dependencies particularly with the United States. A final comprehensive effectiveness assessment is planned for 2027 when a more complete dataset will be available.

title: ESMA launches Common Supervisory Action with NCAs on the risk management function
summary: ESMA is launching a Common Supervisory Action (CSA) in collaboration with National Competent Authorities to assess the risk management function of UCITS management companies and Alternative Investment Fund Managers across the EU, running throughout 2026 and 2027. The CSA will examine governance and organisation of the risk management function, identification and monitoring of risks, and reporting to senior management, using a common assessment framework developed by ESMA. A final report with results is expected to be published in 2028.

title: ESMA reminds firms of existing rules and obligations under binary option measures amid growing popularity of prediction markets globally
summary: ESMA has issued a statement reminding firms that event contracts (prediction markets) with a binary financial outcome may fall within the scope of existing national product intervention measures on binary options, which prohibit their marketing, distribution, or sale to retail clients. Firms are also reminded that distributing such contracts as financial instruments in the EU requires authorisation as an investment firm, even when distributed solely to non-retail clients. The statement is prompted by the growing global popularity of prediction markets and increasing retail participation.

title: ESMA identifies up to €1 billion in potential annual savings from simplifying EU transaction reporting
summary: ESMA has published a final report recommending a 'Report Once' approach to simplify EU transaction reporting across MiFIR, EMIR, and SFTR, estimating annual net savings of €250 million to €1 billion for market participants. The report proposes a staged approach combining immediate burden-reduction measures with a long-term integrated reporting framework, with implementation requiring targeted legislative changes and phased rollout. No compliance deadline is specified; ESMA will next engage with EU institutions on the recommendations.

title: Moody’s Germany fined EUR 2,145,000 for misreporting to ESMA
summary: ESMA has fined Moody's Deutschland GmbH EUR 2,145,000 for four breaches of the Credit Rating Agencies Regulation, relating to failures to provide complete, accurate, and up-to-date data to ESMA and deficiencies in its regulatory reporting framework, including policies, procedures, and internal controls. The errors affected only data submitted to ESMA and published on its central platform, not credit ratings published on Moody's Germany's website. The breaches were found to have resulted from negligence, and ESMA also issued a public notice alongside the fine.

title: ESMA recognises the Clearing Corporation of India Limited as a Tier 1 third-country CCP
summary: ESMA has recognised the Clearing Corporation of India Limited (CCIL) as a Tier 1 third-country central counterparty (CCP) under EMIR, with effect from 30 June 2026. The recognition permits CCIL to provide clearing services to EU clearing members and trading venues, including banks and investment firms, and follows an equivalence decision by the European Commission and a Memorandum of Understanding between ESMA and the Reserve Bank of India. No compliance deadline or required action for regulated entities is stated in the text.

title: ESMA consults on simplifying EU Taxonomy disclosure framework
summary: ESMA has launched a consultation on technical advice to the European Commission on selected KPIs under the Taxonomy Disclosures Delegated Act, proposing simplifications to the EU Taxonomy disclosure framework for non-financial undertakings and asset managers, including changes to the operational expenditure KPI and group-level reporting. The consultation runs for six weeks until 12 August 2026, with a public hearing on 22 July 2026. ESMA will deliver its final technical advice by end-October 2026.

title: ESMA appoints Peter Tkáč as the new member of its Management Board
summary: ESMA has appointed Peter Tkáč of Národná Banka Slovenska (NBS), Slovakia, as a new member of its Management Board, following an election at the Board of Supervisors meeting on 30 June 2026. He replaces outgoing member Ante Žigman of HANFA, whose mandate ended on 19 June 2026. Mr. Tkáč will serve until 31 March 2027, with the possibility of renewal.

title: 2026-008: Critical vulnerabilities in Ivanti Sentry
summary: On 9 June 2026, Ivanti released a security advisory addressing two critical vulnerabilities in Ivanti Sentry: CVE-2026-10520 (CVSS 10, OS Command Injection enabling unauthenticated root-level remote code execution) and CVE-2026-10523 (CVSS 9.9, Authentication Bypass enabling creation of arbitrary administrative accounts). Affected versions are Sentry 10.5.1 and prior, 10.6.1 and prior, and 10.7.0. CERT-EU recommends applying the vendor's fixed versions immediately.
flag_reason: The vulnerabilities are critical and allow unauthenticated remote code execution, but the supplied text does not evidence whether Ivanti Sentry is widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so U-1 cannot be finalised and the item is flagged for human review with a provisional level of High.

title: 2026-007: Critical Vulnerability in Windows Netlogon
summary: CERT-EU has published a security advisory for CVE-2026-41089, a critical (CVSS 9.8) stack-based buffer overflow in Windows Netlogon affecting multiple Windows Server versions acting as domain controllers. An unauthenticated attacker can execute arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges by sending specially crafted network packets, and the Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium confirms the vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild. It is strongly recommended to update all affected Windows Server assets as soon as possible.
flag_reason: The vulnerability meets the critical-and-actively-exploited clause of U-1, but the supplied text does not evidence whether Windows Server domain controllers are widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so client-base relevance cannot be confirmed and the item must be flagged for human review rather than finalised as Urgent.

title: 2026-006: Critical Vulnerability in PAN-OS
summary: On 6 May 2026, CERT-EU published a security advisory regarding CVE-2026-0300, a critical buffer overflow vulnerability (CVSS 9.3) in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS affecting PA-Series and VM-Series firewalls configured to use User-ID Authentication Portal, which allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code with root privileges. Palo Alto has observed limited exploitation of this vulnerability in the wild. Patches are not yet available at time of publication; recommended mitigations include restricting Authentication Portal access to trusted zones or disabling it if not required.
flag_reason: The vulnerability is critical and has observed limited exploitation, potentially satisfying the first clause of U-1, but the supplied text does not evidence whether PAN-OS or Palo Alto PA-Series/VM-Series firewalls are widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so the second clause of U-1 cannot be confirmed and the item cannot be finalised as Urgent.

title: 2026-005: High Vulnerability in the Linux Kernel ("Copy Fail")
summary: On 29 April 2026, CERT-EU published a security advisory for CVE-2026-31431 ('Copy Fail'), a high-severity local privilege escalation vulnerability (CVSS 7.8) in the Linux kernel's algif_aead module, affecting all mainstream Linux distributions shipping a kernel built since 2017. A public proof-of-concept exploit has been released and no distribution has yet shipped a fixed kernel package as of the advisory date. CERT-EU strongly recommends applying an interim mitigation by disabling the algif_aead kernel module immediately, and prioritising Kubernetes nodes and CI/CD runners exposed to untrusted workloads.
flag_reason: The supplied text establishes that a public proof-of-concept exploit exists, satisfying the first clause of U-1, but the text does not evidence whether the affected Linux kernel systems are widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so the second clause of U-1 cannot be confirmed and the item cannot be finalised as Urgent.

title: 2026-004: Critical Vulnerability in SharePoint Exploited
summary: CERT-EU has issued a security advisory regarding CVE-2026-20963, a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint Server, which was added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue on 18 March 2026. Three additional RCE flaws in SharePoint were also addressed in the March 2026 patch release. CERT-EU strongly recommends updating SharePoint servers as soon as possible, prioritising internet-facing assets, and advises remediation steps including enabling AMSI, deploying EDR, rotating ASP.NET machine keys, and conducting a compromise assessment.
flag_reason: The vulnerability is critical and actively exploited (satisfying the first clause of U-1), but the supplied text does not evidence whether Microsoft SharePoint is widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so the second clause of U-1 cannot be confirmed and the item cannot be finalised as Urgent.

title: 2026-003: Multiple Vulnerabilities in Citrix NetScaler and Citrix ADC
summary: On 23 March 2026, CERT-EU published a security advisory covering two vulnerabilities in Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway: CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3, out-of-bounds read enabling sensitive information disclosure on systems configured as SAML IdP) and CVE-2026-4368 (CVSS 7.7, race condition enabling session mix-up on Gateway or AAA virtual server configurations). At the time of publication, there is no public evidence of active exploitation. CERT-EU strongly recommends applying network-level access controls, prioritising remediation of internet-facing appliances, taking snapshots before patching, and terminating all active sessions after patching.

title: 2026-002: Multiple Vulnerabilities in Cisco Products
summary: On 25 February 2026, Cisco released advisories covering multiple high and critical severity vulnerabilities in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN controllers and SD-WAN Manager, including CVE-2026-20127 (CVSS 10), an authentication bypass exploited in the wild since 2023 that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to gain administrative privileges. CERT-EU recommends immediate actions including capturing forensic evidence, hunting for indicators of compromise using the published hunting guide, updating affected devices to fixed versions, and restricting external access to SD-WAN management interfaces. Affected versions span a wide range of SD-WAN software releases prior to the fixed versions specified in the advisory.
flag_reason: CVE-2026-20127 meets the critical-and-actively-exploited clause of U-1, but the supplied text does not evidence whether Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN products are widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so the item cannot be finalised as Urgent and is flagged for human review with a provisional level of High per H-4.

title: 2026-001: Critical vulnerabilities in Ivanti EPMM
summary: On 29 January 2026, Ivanti released a security advisory covering two critical code injection vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-1281 and CVE-2026-1340, both CVSS 9.8) in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) versions 12.7.0.0 and prior, which allow unauthenticated remote code execution; one vulnerability has been exploited in a limited number of cases. CERT-EU recommends securing forensic evidence and applying the vendor hotfix to affected appliances, noting that a permanent fix is planned for release 12.8.0.0 in Q1 2026. The hotfix must be reapplied after any version upgrade as it does not survive the upgrade process.
flag_reason: The supplied text establishes that at least one vulnerability is critical and has been actively exploited, satisfying the first clause of U-1, but the text does not evidence whether Ivanti EPMM is widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so the second clause of U-1 cannot be confirmed and the item cannot be finalised as Urgent.

title: 2025-042: Critical Vulnerability in Cisco Secure Email and Web Manager
summary: On 17 December 2025, Cisco disclosed a critical vulnerability (CVE-2025-20393, CVSS 10) in Cisco Secure Email Gateway and Cisco Secure Email and Web Manager, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands with root privileges on affected appliances when the Spam Quarantine feature is enabled and internet-facing. No patch is currently available. CERT-EU recommends checking whether appliances are exposed, restricting access, opening a Cisco TAC case if compromise is suspected, and investigating any lateral movement.
flag_reason: The vulnerability is critical (CVSS 10) and exploitation is implied by the remediation guidance, but the supplied text does not evidence whether Cisco Secure Email Gateway or Cisco Secure Email and Web Manager are widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so U-1 cannot be finalised and the item is flagged for human review with a provisional level of High.

title: 2025-041: Critical Security Vulnerability in React Server Components
summary: On 3 December 2025, the React Team disclosed a critical vulnerability (CVE-2025-55182, CVSS 10) in React Server Components packages, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution via maliciously crafted HTTP requests due to unsafe deserialisation. Affected versions include react-server-dom-webpack, react-server-dom-parcel, and react-server-dom-turbopack across React versions 19.0 through 19.2.0, with confirmed impact on Next.js App Router, Vite, Parcel, React Router, Redwood SDK, Waku, and any third-party project bundling the vulnerable packages. CERT-EU recommends updating to fixed versions 19.0.1, 19.1.2, or 19.2.1 as soon as possible.
flag_reason: The vulnerability is critical and publicly disclosed with a CVSS score of 10, potentially satisfying the first clause of U-1, but the supplied text does not evidence whether React Server Components are widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so the second clause of U-1 cannot be confirmed and the item cannot be finalised as Urgent.

title: ​The EBA publishes final Guidelines on the authorisation of third-country branches under the Capital Requirements Directive
summary: The EBA has published final Guidelines on the authorisation of third-country credit institutions seeking to establish a third-country branch (TCB) in an EU Member State, as mandated by CRD6. The Guidelines specify the information to be provided, assessment criteria, applicable process, and templates for authorisation applications, and are addressed to competent authorities and applicant third-country head undertakings. No immediate compliance deadline is stated in the text, and the Guidelines form part of a broader TCB framework being progressively delivered since January 2026.

title: ESMA launches Common Supervisory Action on CASPs’ digital operational resilience for custody
summary: ESMA is launching a Common Supervisory Action (CSA) to assess the digital operational resilience frameworks of Crypto-Asset Service Providers (CASPs), with a focus on custody services. The CSA will examine governance arrangements, key and storage management, transaction controls, incident detection and response, smart contract risks, and third-party dependencies, and will be conducted by National Competent Authorities on a risk-based sample of authorised CASPs from the second half of 2026 to the first half of 2027. A final report consolidating NCA findings will be submitted to ESMA's Board of Supervisors in the second half of 2027.

title: CVE-2026-48908: JoomShaper SP Page Builder Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type Vulnerability
summary: CVE-2026-48908 is a critical (CVSS 4.0 score 10.0) unrestricted file upload vulnerability in JoomShaper SP Page Builder for Joomla, affecting versions up to 6.6.1, which allows unauthenticated remote attackers to upload and execute arbitrary PHP code. CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog on 7 July 2026 with a due date of 10 July 2026, requiring affected parties to apply vendor mitigations or discontinue use of the product. Exploitation is confirmed as active and the vulnerability is rated automatable with total technical impact.
flag_reason: The vulnerability is critical and actively exploited (satisfying the first clause of U-1), but the supplied text does not evidence that SP Page Builder for Joomla is widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so U-1 cannot be finalised and the item is flagged for human review with a provisional level of High under H-4.

title: CVE-2026-55255: Langflow Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key Vulnerability
summary: CVE-2026-55255 is an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability in Langflow, a tool for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows, affecting versions prior to 1.9.1; it allows an authenticated attacker to execute any flow belonging to another user by specifying the victim's flow ID. The vulnerability is listed in CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog with active exploitation confirmed, a CVSS 3.x base score of 8.4 (HIGH), and a remediation due date of 10 July 2026. Required action is to apply vendor mitigations or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
flag_reason: The vulnerability is confirmed critical and actively exploited (satisfying the first clause of U-1), but the supplied text does not evidence whether Langflow is widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so the second clause of U-1 cannot be confirmed and the item must be flagged for human review rather than finalised as Urgent.

title: CVE-2026-56290: Joomlack Page Builder Improper Access Control Vulnerability
summary: CVE-2026-56290 is a critical (CVSS 4.0: 10.0, CVSS 3.1: 9.8) improper access control vulnerability in the Joomlack Page Builder CK extension for Joomla, allowing unauthenticated arbitrary file upload leading to full remote code execution. CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog on 7 July 2026, with exploitation confirmed as active and the vulnerability rated automatable with total technical impact. The required action deadline is 10 July 2026, directing affected parties to apply vendor mitigations or discontinue use of the product.
flag_reason: The vulnerability meets the critical-and-actively-exploited clause of U-1, but the supplied text does not evidence whether Joomlack Page Builder CK is widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so the item cannot be finalised as Urgent and is flagged for human review with a provisional level of High under H-4.

title: CVE-2026-48282: Adobe ColdFusion Path Traversal Vulnerability
summary: Adobe ColdFusion versions 2025.9, 2023.20 and earlier contain a critical path traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-48282) with a CVSS 3.x base score of 10.0, enabling unauthenticated remote arbitrary code execution without user interaction. CISA has added this to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog, confirming active exploitation, and has set a remediation due date of 10 July 2026, requiring affected organisations to apply vendor mitigations or discontinue use of the product. The supplied text does not evidence whether Adobe ColdFusion is widely used by Finalogic or its client base.
flag_reason: The vulnerability is critical and actively exploited (U-1 first clause met), but the supplied text does not evidence that Adobe ColdFusion is widely used by Finalogic or its client base, so U-1 cannot be finalised and the item must be routed to human review with a provisional level of Urgent.

title: ​The EBA releases the final technical package for its 4.3 reporting framework to support Third-Country Branches reporting and AML Authority risk assessment data collection
summary: The European Banking Authority has released the final technical package for version 4.3 of its reporting framework. This package supports new reporting requirements for Third-Country Branches under the Capital Requirements Directive, starting 31 March 2027, and risk assessment data collection for the Anti-Money Laundering Authority, starting 31 December 2026.

title: ​The EBA issues Opinion on the implementation of IFRS 18 in supervisory financial reporting to support consistency with IFRS requirements
summary: The European Banking Authority has issued an Opinion providing guidance on supervisory financial reporting during the transition to IFRS 18. The guidance advises competent authorities to allow institutions to voluntarily use IFRS 18-aligned templates during the interim period between January 2027 and September 2027. This measure aims to prevent the operational burden of maintaining dual reporting formats.

title: New Q&As available
summary: The European Securities and Markets Authority has published new Questions and Answers clarifying aspects of several EU regulations. The updates provide guidance on the EU ESG Ratings Regulation, the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation, and the Markets in Financial Instruments Regulation. Key topics addressed include notification periods, crypto-asset custody services, and consolidated tape provider regimes.
